g.text: where they apply (self-attn branch; FFN branch is identical
gate_* are initialized near zero, so a fresh block starts as the identity (x passes through) and
learns to add its contribution — like adaLN-Zero, but the modulation is a cheap learned
table plus the timestep, not a per-block MLP.
modulate(y,sh,sc)=y·(1+sc)+sh is applied after norm1 / norm2; gate(x,g,branch)=x+g·branch wraps the self-attn and FFN residuals.
six = (modulation_table[1,6,1536] + t_mod[B,6,1536]).chunk(6) → shift_msa, scale_msa, gate_msa, shift_mlp, scale_mlp, gate_mlp.
Wan2.1-VACE-1.3B · 7 · AdaLN-single · 6 modulation params per block from table + timestep
